title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A psychological thriller about a woman who shoots her husband and then stops speaking, leaving a mystery that unfolds through therapy sessions.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: A practical guide on how small habits can lead to remarkable results through consistent improvement and behavioral systems.

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling vision of a totalitarian future where surveillance and propaganda control every aspect of life.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful story about racial injustice and moral growth in the American South, seen through a child’s eyes.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: A fantasy adventure following Bilbo Baggins as he embarks on a journey filled with dragons, treasure, and unexpected courage.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A must-read for developers that teaches principles and best practices for writing clean, maintainable code.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical tale about following your dreams and listening to your heart through the journey of a shepherd boy.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: An exploration of human history, from the evolution of Homo sapiens to modern societies and future possibilities.

title: The Maidens
author: Alex Michaelides
description: A psychological thriller set in a university where a professor becomes the prime suspect in a series of murders.

title: The Power of Habit
author: Charles Duhigg
description: An insightful book explaining the science behind habits and how they can be changed to improve productivity and life.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: A dystopian novel depicting a technologically advanced society driven by pleasure, control, and conditioning.

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A romantic classic exploring themes of love, class, and misunderstandings in 19th-century England.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
description: The first book in the Harry Potter series, introducing a young wizard discovering his magical heritage.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt
description: A classic programming book offering practical advice for software developers to improve their craft.
